Long, long ago, far before the sky had a voice, the young world was cloaked in an unbroken, heavy silence. The mountains stood like mute giants, and the rivers flowed without a single murmur. The Creator, a being woven from starlight and shadow, found this silence unbearable. Plucking a single silver thread from the fabric of the cosmos, the Creator fashioned a magnificent bird with feathers of iridescent blue and eyes like polished obsidian. This was Aethelgard, the First Singer.

The Creator whispered a secret into Aethelgard’s ear and tossed him into the windless air. As the bird beat his wings, the first breeze was born, rustling the leaves of the ancient forests. Then, Aethelgard opened his beak and sang. The melody was so pure and piercing that it shattered the crystalline dome of the sky, causing fragments of sound to rain down upon the earth. Where these fragments landed, life found its voice. The wolves learned to howl at the moon, the oceans learned to crash against the shore, and humanity learned to speak. Even to this very day, when the autumn wind howls fiercely through the deep valleys, it is merely the lingering echo of Aethelgard’s first song.

Long ago, before the mountains had names, there lived a spirit named Kaelen. Kaelen possessed a voice of liquid gold, capable of weaving melodies that caused the spring flowers to bloom and the winter snows to melt. He sang from the highest peaks, his songs a gift to the valley below.

But Zephyros, the Great North Wind, grew fiercely jealous. Zephyros believed the sky belonged solely to his howling gales, not to the gentle tunes of a mountain spirit. One evening, as Kaelen began his twilight ballad, Zephyros descended in a violent fury. He stole Kaelen’s breath, trapping it within the hollows of the jagged rocks, and cursed the spirit to never speak a single original word again.

Stripped of his voice, Kaelen faded into the stone itself, becoming one with the deep canyons and towering cliffs. Yet, the spirit's desire to connect remained unbroken. Unable to sing his own songs, Kaelen learned to catch the words of travelers and animals, calling them back as a reminder that he was still listening. 

Today, we call him Echo. Whenever you dare to shout into a deep, hollow canyon, it is Kaelen, forever answering you, forever longing to sing once more.
